hero.headline: Stay on top of your money — not buried in spreadsheets.
hero.subhead: Nixus automates the upkeep: upload a credit card statement, get categorized expenses, see your next move. Budget, TFSA, RRSP, net worth — on your machine, without Plaid.
pillars.heading: Rearview apps show your money. Nixus helps you stay on top of it.
pillars.subhead: Built for spreadsheet trackers who won't hand bank credentials to the cloud.
pillars.automation.title: Automation without bank access
pillars.automation.description: Upload screenshots or PDFs — no Plaid, no passwords. AI extracts and categorizes in seconds.
pillars.nextAction.title: Next action, not another chart
pillars.nextAction.description: Emergency fund runway, savings capacity, and one clear priority — not just dashboards.
pillars.lifeUpkeep.title: One app for life upkeep
pillars.lifeUpkeep.description: Finance and car maintenance today — same shell, same local data. More modules over time.
beta.limitations.heading: What Nixus is — and isn't
beta.limitations.intro: Before you download: an honest list so you know if this is worth your time.
beta.limitations.items.noBankSync: No bank connection — you upload credit card statements manually (screenshot or PDF).
beta.limitations.items.desktopOnly: Desktop only — macOS and Windows. No mobile app.
beta.limitations.items.singleUser: Single user — one person's finances per install.
beta.limitations.items.aiCredentials: AI import requires your own API credentials (stored in your OS keychain).
beta.limitations.items.preAlphaStability: Pre-alpha — features change and things break between releases.
beta.limitations.items.notProfessionalAd: Not tax, legal, or investment advice — a tracking tool, not a professional service.
beta.invite.heading: Help shape Nixus
beta.invite.body: I'm looking for a handful of people who still track personal finances in a spreadsheet and are willing to use Nixus for a few weeks and tell me what's confusing or broken. I built this for myself — I'm not asking you to promote it, just honest friction reports.
betaPage.meta.title: Nixus beta — try the pre-alpha personal finance app
betaPage.meta.description: Local-first desktop app for spreadsheet users. Honest limitations, download link, and how to give feedback by email.
betaPage.hero.heading: Try Nixus — honest preview
betaPage.hero.lead: A local financial copilot for spreadsheet users — automate statement import, get next-action guidance, and keep everything on your machine. I'm looking for a handful of testers willing to use it for a few weeks and tell me what's confusing or broken.
betaPage.fit.heading: Is this for you?
betaPage.fit.good.heading: Good fit if you…
betaPage.fit.good.spreadsheet: Track personal finances in a spreadsheet today
betaPage.fit.good.desktop: Use macOS or Windows on a desktop or laptop
betaPage.fit.good.upload: Can upload credit card statements (screenshot or PDF) instead of bank sync
betaPage.fit.good.roughEdges: Are okay with rough edges and will tell me what breaks
betaPage.fit.bad.heading: Not for you if you…
betaPage.fit.bad.advice: Need tax, legal, or investment advice

betaPage.screenshots.budget.caption: Monthly budget at a glance — category groups and spending vs. plan.

betaPage.screenshots.aiImport.caption: Ask budget questions in plain language — the AI uses your real numbers.
betaPage.screenshots.aiImport.alt: Nixus AI chat answering a monthly budget check-in
betaPage.screenshots.netWorth.caption: Accounts, investments, and assets in a single view over time.
betaPage.getStarted.heading: Get started
betaPage.getStarted.download.title: Download
betaPage.getStarted.download.body: Click the button above for your platform (macOS or Windows).
betaPage.getStarted.install.title: Install
betaPage.getStarted.install.body: macOS: if Gatekeeper blocks the app, open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Open Anyway. Windows: if SmartScreen appears, click More info → Run anyway.
betaPage.getStarted.installHelpLink: Full install help on the homepage →
betaPage.getStarted.firstOpen.title: Open Nixus
betaPage.getStarted.firstOpen.body: No web signup — your data stays on your machine from the first launch.
betaPage.feedback.heading: How to give feedback
betaPage.feedback.body: No Discord or chat app required. Email works — I read everything personally. This is pre-alpha, not a support desk.
betaPage.feedback.prompt: Most useful question: "What almost made you close it in the first 10 minutes?"
betaPage.feedback.detail: You don't need a bug report template or screenshots — just tell me what felt wrong.
betaPage.faq.heading: Quick questions
aiDemo.heading: Statement in. Categorized out.
aiDemo.subhead: The monthly chore you dread — compressed to a few seconds. No bank login required.
aiDemo.figureAria: AI parsing demo: a credit card statement becomes categorized expenses in seconds
features.heading: A financial copilot on your machine
features.subhead: Deterministic insights where it matters. AI where it helps. Confirm before anything changes.
features.aiImport.title: Document Automation
features.aiImport.description: CC screenshots or PDFs → categorized expenses. No bank sync required.
features.financialHealth.title: Financial Health
features.financialHealth.description: Runway, savings rate, and one priority action — rule-based, not guesswork.
features.budget.title: Budget Builder
features.budget.description: Monthly budgets with category groups. See where you stand at a glance.
features.accounts.title: Canadian Accounts
features.accounts.description: Chequing, credit cards, TFSA, RRSP, USD — one view built for Canada.
features.netWorth.title: Net Worth History
features.netWorth.description: Track cash, housing, crypto, and investments over time — by category.
features.aiChat.title: AI Copilot
features.aiChat.description: Ask in plain language. Proposed changes wait for your confirmation.
features.garage.title: Garage Module
features.garage.description: Maintenance schedules, service history, and due dates for every vehicle.
features.localFirst.title: Local-First Privacy
features.localFirst.description: SQLite on your machine. Open source. Your finances never touch our servers.
faq.heading: Frequently asked
faq.bankConnection.question: Does this connect to my bank?
faq.bankConnection.answer: No, by design — unlike Monarch, YNAB, and most Mint replacements. You upload a credit card statement (screenshot or PDF) and the AI parses it. Nixus never asks for your bank login, never connects to Plaid, and doesn't pull data from your accounts. You stay in control.
faq.installSafety.question: Is this safe to install?
faq.installSafety.answer: Nixus is open source — you can inspect the code on GitHub. First-launch warnings (macOS Gatekeeper, Windows SmartScreen) are normal for apps not yet signed by Apple or trusted by Microsoft's reputation system. Install instructions appear after you click Download.
faq.isItFree.question: Is it free?
faq.isItFree.answer: Yes, free for now. The plan is a free tier plus paid modules later — the AI import feature is the most likely first paid module. When that ships, existing users will see clear pricing — no surprise charges.
faq.dataStorage.question: Where is my data stored?
faq.dataStorage.answer: Locally on your machine. The AI feature sends a credit card statement to AWS Bedrock for parsing only — that request is one-shot and isn't retained. Your budget data, accounts, and net worth never leave your computer.
faq.linuxSupport.question: Is Linux supported?
faq.linuxSupport.answer: Not in the alpha version. Linux support is on the roadmap but not committed. The macOS build may run via compatibility layers but isn't officially tested.
faq.mobileSupport.question: What about mobile?
faq.mobileSupport.answer: Nixus is a desktop app (macOS and Windows). There's no mobile app planned for the alpha version — the focus is on getting the desktop experience right first.
faq.whoBuilt.question: Who built this?
faq.whoBuilt.answer: One person — built because spreadsheets stopped scaling for my own finances. You can find me on GitHub, or reach out by email.
faq.howToUpdate.question: How do I update?
faq.howToUpdate.answer: Nothing to do — Nixus checks for updates automatically and installs them in the background. You'll always have the latest version next time you open the app.
faq.preAlpha.question: What does 'pre-alpha' mean?
faq.preAlpha.answer: Nixus is in pre-alpha: the core functionality is in place, and the product is actively evolving based on user feedback and where we take it from here. Things may change between releases. Your data stays local, and auto-update keeps you on the latest version.
preAlpha.banner.message: Nixus is in pre-alpha — the product is still maturing and your feedback shapes what comes next.
downloadBanner.heading: Download started
downloadBanner.macosBody: When macOS shows "unidentified developer," open System Settings → Privacy & Security and click Open Anyway. Normal for apps outside the Mac App Store.
downloadBanner.windowsBody: When SmartScreen appears, click More info → Run anyway to install the .exe. Expected for new apps until they build a Microsoft trust score.
installInstructions.heading: Your Nixus download is starting
installInstructions.intro: Pick your platform below for the one extra click most desktop apps need on first launch.
installInstructions.macosBody: Once the .dmg finishes downloading, double-click it to open. The first launch shows "Nixus is from an unidentified developer." Open System Settings → Privacy & Security, scroll to the bottom, and click Open Anyway. This is normal for apps not distributed through the Mac App Store.
installInstructions.windowsBody: Once the .msi finishes downloading, run it. Windows SmartScreen may warn that the publisher isn't recognized. Click More info → Run anyway to install. This is expected for new apps until they build a Microsoft reputation score.
download.visitOnDesktop: Visit on a Mac or PC to download
download.emailSubject: Try Nixus on your Mac or PC
